key_insight: Noble gas fluorides can act as strong noncontact electron acceptors that drive exceptionally large hole transfer in TMD van der Waals heterostructures without electric gating.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a chemical route to tune carrier density and potentially correlated phases in atomically thin semiconductors using interlayer charge transfer rather than external electrodes.
method: Screened transition metal dichalcogenides and electron acceptors for favorable band alignment.
Used DFT to model TMD/hBN/oxidizer heterostructures.
Computed periodic large-supercell charge transfer for WS2/hBN/XeF2 and WSe2/hBN/KrF2.
evidence_signal: DFT results showed hole concentrations up to 0.23 h+ per W atom in WS2/hBN/XeF2 and 0.35 h+ per W atom in WSe2/hBN/KrF2.
watch_item: The approach relies on specific band alignment and chemically reactive oxidizers, so practical synthesis, stability, and interface control may limit implementation.
paper_title: Huge hole injection in tungsten dichalcogenide heterostructures without electric gating: a DFT study
